description: Voice modules for cold outreach. Each archetype defines who the buyer is, what they care about, and how to write to them.

archetypes.display_name: Care sector activities coordinator or home manager
what_they_care_about: Reliability — cancellations hurt them with residents
DBS checked, insured, experienced with elderly or dementia clients
language_rules: Use residents never clients or customers
Lead with benefit to residents not service features
Mention DBS insurance and experience with elderly clients if true
Acknowledge the trust involved in letting someone into their home
archetypes.cta_style: low commitment — suggest a visit or quick call, no pressure

archetypes.display_name: Education administrator — bursar, head of boarding, nursery manager
what_they_care_about: Minimal disruption to the school day
Value for parents or the school budget
archetypes.tone: polite, formal-ish, safeguarding-aware, confident not pushy
language_rules: Mention Enhanced DBS safeguarding training and insurance up front if applicable
Reference the school by name
Keep emails short
archetypes.cta_style: offer to send a one-page info sheet or meet briefly after the school day

what_they_care_about: Being able to say yes to unusual guest requests
what_they_dont_care_about: Anything that sounds like a franchise pitch
language_rules: Use guests never customers
Reference a specific offering of theirs if known from enrichment
Keep it short under 90 words
archetypes.cta_style: offer preferred supplier trial or taster session

what_they_care_about: Easy to run perks that dont create admin
Fitting within existing wellness budget lines
language_rules: Lead with the employee experience outcome not your service
Mention how easy it is to run
Offer a trial not a contract
archetypes.cta_style: offer a free taster day for the team

what_they_care_about: Fixed costs they can plan around
Reliable suppliers who turn up on time
Not having to chase invoices or insurance certs
language_rules: Lead with a concrete hook — what you do and where
Mention insurance and accreditation specifically if relevant
Be upfront about what you offer
Keep it short — operations people skim
archetypes.cta_style: offer a one-site trial or quick call to discuss requirements

what_they_care_about: Not having to field more complaints
Services that add value to the building
language_rules: Frame as a resident amenity not a sale
Mention vetting insurance and reliability
Reference similar buildings served if true
archetypes.cta_style: offer to be added to the buildings approved contractor list

language_rules: Lead with qualifications and registration if applicable
Mention any prior NHS or private practice experience
Keep it brief and professional
archetypes.cta_style: offer a short call to discuss how you could support their patients
